Package org.eventb.ui.manipulation

Interface IAttributeManipulation

All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractAttributeManipulation, AbstractBooleanManipulation, AbstractContextManipulation, AssignmentAttributeManipulation, CommentAttributeManipulation, ConvergenceAttributeManipulation, ExpressionAttributeManipulation, ExtendedAttributeManipulation, ExtendsContextAbstractContextNameAttributeManipulation, IdentifierAttributeManipulation, LabelAttributeManipulation, NullAttributeManipulation, PredicateAttributeManipulation, RefinesEventAbstractEventLabelAttributeManipulation, RefinesMachineAbstractMachineNameAttributeManipulation, SeesContextNameAttributeManipulation, TheoremAttributeManipulation
public interface IAttributeManipulation
Common protocol for manipulating attributes in the Event-B UI. Implementations of this interface should be contributed to the org.eventb.ui.editorItems extension point in either of the following elements:
  • choiceAttribute,
  • textAttribute,
  • toggleAttribute.
Since:
3.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    String[]
    getPossibleValues(IRodinElement element, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor)

    Returns the value of the attribute (as a String) of the given element.

    Sets the value of the attribute for the given element to some default.
    void

    Sets the value of the attribute of the given element from a String.

  • Method Details

    • setDefaultValue

      void setDefaultValue(IRodinElement element, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Sets the value of the attribute for the given element to some default. This method is called at widget creation or when the user selects the UNDEFINED pseudo-entry in a combo-box.
      Parameters:
      element - an internal element
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting and cancellation are not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if some problem occurred

    • hasValue

      boolean hasValue(IRodinElement element, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Returns true if the given element has the attribute.
      Parameters:
      element - an internal element
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting and cancellation are not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if some problem occurred

    • getValue

      String getValue(IRodinElement element, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Returns the value of the attribute (as a String) of the given element.
      Parameters:
      element - an internal element
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting and cancellation are not desired
      Returns:
      the string value of the attribute
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if some problem occurred

    • setValue

      void setValue(IRodinElement element, String value, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Sets the value of the attribute of the given element from a String.
      Parameters:
      element - an internal element
      value - string value of the attribute
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting and cancellation are not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if some problem occurred

    • removeAttribute

      void removeAttribute(IRodinElement element, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Removes the attribute from the given element.
      Parameters:
      element - an internal element
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting and cancellation are not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if some problem occurred

    • getPossibleValues

      String[] getPossibleValues(IRodinElement element, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor)
      Gets the possible values of the attribute for the given element. This is used for filling the list of a combo-box. Returns null if the attribute is not intended to be edited by a combo-box widget.

      The returned list must include the current value of the attribute, otherwise the display of the combo box will not be accurate on Linux and Mac OS X.

      Parameters:
      element - an internal element
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting and cancellation are not desired
      Returns:
      an array of all values that the attribute can take, or null if unknown
